Detailed Description

Internal VM implementation of the standard TimeZone

Constructor & Destructor Documentation

jelatine::VMTimeZone::VMTimeZone ( int  rawOffset,
String  id 
) [inline]

Create a SimpleTimeZone with the given time offset from GMT and without daylight savings.

Parameters:
rawOffset the time offset from GMT in milliseconds.
id The identifier of this time zone.

Definition at line 189 of file VMTimeZone.java.

Member Function Documentation

int jelatine::VMTimeZone::getDSTSavings (  )  [inline]

Gets the daylight savings offset. This is a positive offset in milliseconds with respect to standard time. Typically this is one hour, but for some time zones this may be half an our.

Returns:
the daylight savings offset in milliseconds.

Definition at line 556 of file VMTimeZone.java.

int jelatine::VMTimeZone::getOffset ( int  era,
int  year,
int  month,
int  day,
int  dayOfWeek,
int  millis 
) [inline, virtual]

Gets the time zone offset, for current date, modified in case of daylight savings. This is the offset to add to UTC to get the local time.

In the standard JDK the results given by this method may result in inaccurate results at the end of February or the beginning of March. To avoid this, you should use Calendar instead: offset = cal.get(Calendar.ZONE_OFFSET) + cal.get(Calendar.DST_OFFSET);

This version doesn't suffer this inaccuracy.

The arguments don't follow the approach for setting start and end rules. The day must be a positive number and dayOfWeek must be a positive value from Calendar. dayOfWeek is redundant, but must match the other values or an inaccurate result may be returned.

Parameters:
era the era of the given date
year the year of the given date
month the month of the given date, 0 for January.
day the day of month
dayOfWeek the day of week; this must match the other fields.
millis the millis in the day (in local standard time)
Returns:
the time zone offset in milliseconds.
Exceptions:
IllegalArgumentException if arguments are incorrect.

Implements java::util::TimeZone.

Definition at line 487 of file VMTimeZone.java.

int jelatine::VMTimeZone::getRawOffset (  )  [inline, virtual]

Returns the time zone offset to GMT in milliseconds, ignoring day light savings.

Returns:
the time zone offset.

Implements java::util::TimeZone.

Definition at line 536 of file VMTimeZone.java.

synchronized int jelatine::VMTimeZone::hashCode (  )  [inline]

Generates the hashCode for the SimpleDateFormat object. It is the rawOffset, possibly, if useDaylightSavings is true, xored with startYear, startMonth, startDayOfWeekInMonth, ..., endTime.

Definition at line 716 of file VMTimeZone.java.

void jelatine::VMTimeZone::setDSTSavings ( int  dstSavings  )  [inline]

Sets the daylight savings offset. This is a positive offset in milliseconds with respect to standard time.

Parameters:
dstSavings the daylight savings offset in milliseconds.

Definition at line 567 of file VMTimeZone.java.

void jelatine::VMTimeZone::setEndRule ( int  month,
int  day,
int  time 
) [inline]

Sets the daylight savings end rule. You must also set the start rule with setStartRule or the result of getOffset is undefined. For the parameters see the ten-argument constructor above.

Parameters:
month The end month of daylight savings.
day A day in month, or a day of week in month.
time A time in millis in standard time.

Definition at line 455 of file VMTimeZone.java.

References setEndRule().

void jelatine::VMTimeZone::setEndRule ( int  month,
int  day,
int  dayOfWeek,
int  time 
) [inline]

Sets the daylight savings end rule. You must also set the start rule with setStartRule or the result of getOffset is undefined. For the parameters see the ten-argument constructor above.

Parameters:
month The end month of daylight savings.
day A day in month, or a day of week in month.
dayOfWeek A day of week, when daylight savings ends.
time A time in millis in standard time.

Definition at line 370 of file VMTimeZone.java.

References STANDARD_TIME, and WALL_TIME.

Referenced by setEndRule().

void jelatine::VMTimeZone::setEndRule ( int  month,
int  day,
int  dayOfWeek,
int  time,
boolean  after 
) [inline]

Sets the daylight savings end rule. You must also set the start rule with setStartRule or the result of getOffset is undefined. For the parameters see the ten-argument constructor above.

Note that this API isn't incredibly well specified. It appears that the after flag must override the parameters, since normally, the day and dayofweek can select this. I.e., if day < 0 and dayOfWeek < 0, on or before mode is chosen. But if after == true, this implementation overrides the signs of the other arguments. And if dayOfWeek == 0, it falls back to the behavior in the other APIs. I guess this should be checked against Sun's implementation.

Parameters:
month The end month of daylight savings.
day A day in month, or a day of week in month.
dayOfWeek A day of week, when daylight savings ends.
time A time in millis in standard time.
after If true, day and dayOfWeek specify first day of week on or after day, else first day of week on or before.

Definition at line 414 of file VMTimeZone.java.

References STANDARD_TIME, and WALL_TIME.

void jelatine::VMTimeZone::setRawOffset ( int  rawOffset  )  [inline]

Sets the standard time zone offset to GMT.

Parameters:
rawOffset The time offset from GMT in milliseconds.

Definition at line 545 of file VMTimeZone.java.

void jelatine::VMTimeZone::setStartRule ( int  month,
int  day,
int  dayOfWeek,
int  time 
) [inline]

Sets the daylight savings start rule. You must also set the end rule with setEndRule or the result of getOffset is undefined. For the parameters see the ten-argument constructor above.

Parameters:
month The month where daylight savings start, zero based. You should use the constants in Calendar.
day A day of month or day of week in month.
dayOfWeek The day of week where daylight savings start.
time The time in milliseconds standard time where daylight savings start.
Exceptions:
IllegalArgumentException if parameters are out of range.

Definition at line 268 of file VMTimeZone.java.

References STANDARD_TIME, and WALL_TIME.

void jelatine::VMTimeZone::setStartRule ( int  month,
int  day,
int  time 
) [inline]

Sets the daylight savings start rule. You must also set the end rule with setEndRule or the result of getOffset is undefined. For the parameters see the ten-argument constructor above.

Parameters:
month The month where daylight savings start, zero based. You should use the constants in Calendar.
day A day of month or day of week in month.
time The time in milliseconds standard time where daylight savings start.

Definition at line 354 of file VMTimeZone.java.

References setStartRule().

void jelatine::VMTimeZone::setStartRule ( int  month,
int  day,
int  dayOfWeek,
int  time,
boolean  after 
) [inline]

Sets the daylight savings start rule. You must also set the end rule with setEndRule or the result of getOffset is undefined. For the parameters see the ten-argument constructor above.

Note that this API isn't incredibly well specified. It appears that the after flag must override the parameters, since normally, the day and dayofweek can select this. I.e., if day < 0 and dayOfWeek < 0, on or before mode is chosen. But if after == true, this implementation overrides the signs of the other arguments. And if dayOfWeek == 0, it falls back to the behavior in the other APIs. I guess this should be checked against Sun's implementation.

Parameters:
month The month where daylight savings start, zero based. You should use the constants in Calendar.
day A day of month or day of week in month.
dayOfWeek The day of week where daylight savings start.
time The time in milliseconds standard time where daylight savings start.
after If true, day and dayOfWeek specify first day of week on or after day, else first day of week on or before.

Definition at line 312 of file VMTimeZone.java.

References STANDARD_TIME, and WALL_TIME.

void jelatine::VMTimeZone::setStartYear ( int  year  )  [inline]

Sets the first year, where daylight savings applies. The daylight savings rule never apply for years in the BC era. Note that this is gregorian calendar specific.

Parameters:
year the start year.

Returns a string representation of this SimpleTimeZone object.

a string representation of this SimpleTimeZone object.

boolean jelatine::VMTimeZone::useDaylightTime (  )  [inline, virtual]

Returns if this time zone uses daylight savings time.

Returns:
true, if we use daylight savings time, false otherwise.

Implements java::util::TimeZone.

Field Documentation

final int jelatine::VMTimeZone::STANDARD_TIME = 1 [static]

Constant to indicate that start and end times are specified in standard time, without adjusting for daylight savings.

Definition at line 170 of file VMTimeZone.java.

Referenced by setEndRule(), and setStartRule().

final int jelatine::VMTimeZone::UTC_TIME = 2 [static]

Constant to indicate that start and end times are specified in UTC.

Definition at line 181 of file VMTimeZone.java.

final int jelatine::VMTimeZone::WALL_TIME = 0 [static]

Constant to indicate that start and end times are specified in wall time, adjusting for daylight savings. This is the default.
